description: Sprint 5 task_40. The 'ctrl-c saves state' promise (product spec §6.11.5). Test by dispatching a synthetic SIGINT during a long-running step and verifying state.json on disk after Runner.run.
input: Flow with one slow prompt step. MockProvider.invoke hangs for 10s. Start Runner.run; after 100ms, call process.emit('SIGINT'). Await the returned promise.
expected_output: Runner.run resolves (or rejects) with RunResult indicating aborted status. state.json on disk has status='aborted'. The running step has been swept to 'failed' with errorMessage 'run aborted'.

name: SIGTERM produces the same abort behavior as SIGINT
description: Sprint 5 task_40. SIGTERM is how most CI systems kill jobs. The Runner must treat it identically to SIGINT — persist state, mark aborted, exit cleanly.
input: Same flow as ABORT-001. Emit SIGTERM instead of SIGINT.
expected_output: Same final state: status='aborted', state.json persisted, running step swept to failed.

name: Abort cascades ctx.abortSignal to every in-flight provider.invoke and child_process
description: Sprint 5 task_40. A top-level AbortController is the aborting mechanism. When aborted, it signals every derived signal. Tests via spies on MockProvider.invoke abortSignal handlers.
input: Flow with two parallel prompt steps. MockProvider spies on ctx.abortSignal for each invocation, registering an 'abort' listener. Emit SIGINT.
expected_output: Both signal listeners fired. Both invocations aborted (invoke rejects with a 'aborted' or AbortError).

name: Runner removes SIGINT/SIGTERM listeners on run completion to avoid leaks across sequential runs
description: Sprint 5 task_40. If listeners leak, a second Runner.run() would register a second listener — SIGINT then fires both, aborting the second run with stale state from the first.
input: Snapshot process.listeners('SIGINT').length before Runner.run. Run a simple flow to completion. Snapshot again.
expected_output: Before and after counts are equal. No net listeners leaked.

name: Runner calls provider.close?() on every provider used in the run (in success and abort paths)
description: Sprint 5 task_40. Providers may hold long-lived resources (sockets, child processes). close() must be called exactly once per provider per run, regardless of run outcome.
input: Two subcases. Case A: happy-path run. Case B: SIGINT abort mid-run. MockProvider with a close() spy. Registry has two distinct MockProviders; flow uses both (heterogeneous step.provider).
expected_output: Both cases: each MockProvider's close() was called exactly 1 time after the run completed/aborted.

name: Runner.resume re-runs ONLY pending and failed steps — succeeded steps are not re-invoked
description: Sprint 5 task_41. The core resume promise (product spec §1.1 'Claude forgot' moment). A step that already succeeded must keep its state on resume, with no second provider.invoke call.
input: Pre-populate runDir with state.json where 'a'=succeeded, 'b'=failed. flow-ref.json written. Re-import flow. MockProvider spy. Call Runner.resume(runDir).
expected_output: MockProvider.invoke was called with ctx.stepId='b' only (not 'a'). Final state: a=succeeded, b=succeeded (assuming the mock returns ok). RunResult.status='succeeded'.

name: Runner.resume reinitializes CostTracker from metrics.json so spent-so-far is accurate
description: Sprint 5 task_41. The pre-run 'spent so far' banner (product spec §6.7) needs the cost from the pre-crash portion. metrics.json must be loaded and the CostTracker seeded.
input: Pre-populate metrics.json with 2 StepMetrics (totalUsd=0.05). Resume a run. After resume completes (with one more step added), inspect CostTracker.summary().
expected_output: summary().totalUsd includes the pre-crash 0.05 PLUS whatever the resumed step spent.

name: Runner.resume loads flow-ref.json and re-imports the flow module
description: Sprint 5 task_41. flow-ref.json is written at run start and tells resume where the flow.ts lives. Without it, resume can't validate or re-execute.
input: Pre-populate runDir with state.json + flow-ref.json pointing at a real flow file. Call Runner.resume(runDir).
expected_output: resume invokes the flow's steps correctly. If flow-ref.json is missing, resume returns a clear error ('flow-ref.json missing — cannot resume').

name: Runner.resume refuses on flowName mismatch and instructs the user to start over
description: Sprint 5 task_41 + §4.8.2 step 2. State written by flow X cannot be resumed by flow Y. The error must be unambiguous so the CLI can format product spec §6.7's resume-refused copy.
input: state.json with flowName='oldFlow'. Re-import flow named 'newFlow'. Runner.resume(runDir) with that flow.
expected_output: Returns err or rejects with StateVersionMismatchError (via loadAndVerify). Error message mentions both 'oldFlow' and 'newFlow' and tells the user to start a new run. No provider.invoke call happens.

name: Runner.resume preserves retry attempts — a step with attempts:2 on a failed resumes with 1 retry remaining (given maxRetries:3)
description: Sprint 5 task_41 + resetStep semantics (STATE-007). Retry budgets must survive crashes. If a step burned 2 of 3 attempts before a crash, resume must only allow 1 more attempt, not 3 fresh ones.
input: state.json with step 'a': { status:'failed', attempts:2 }. Flow defines step a with maxRetries:3. MockProvider always returns err. Runner.resume.
expected_output: MockProvider.invoke was called exactly 1 more time (attempt 3 of 3). After that, run ends with a='failed' and run status='failed'. Not 4 invocations, not 3.

name: Runner.resume recomputes the ready queue from the DAG — succeeded steps unblock their descendants immediately
description: Sprint 5 task_41. Resume's ready queue must seed with steps whose ancestors are all succeeded. A dependent of a pre-crash-succeeded step must run first on resume, not wait for its predecessor to be re-executed.
input: Flow a→b→c. state.json: a=succeeded, b=failed (or pending), c=pending. Resume.
expected_output: First dispatch on resume is step 'b' (not 'a'). After b succeeds, c dispatches. Eventually all three are succeeded with one execution each beyond the pre-crash work.
